recipes.name: add-employee-to-group
recipes.goal: Add an existing employee/client to a named group for a given period.

steps.prompt: Ask the user which group the employee should be added to (the group name).
steps.description: the name of the group the employee should be added to

steps.note: Find the target group. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching group automatically when exactly one group matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the employee (client) to add to the group.
steps.description: the full name (first and last name) of the employee to add

steps.note: Find the employee. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching employee automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the group membership should be valid (valid-until is optional).
steps.description: the start date from which the group membership is valid

steps.note: Add the employee to the group now. clientId, groupId and validFrom are supplied — do not ask for them again. This skill rejects duplicates, so it safely refuses if the employee is already in the group.

recipes.name: bulk-add-employees-to-group
recipes.goal: Bulk-add all employees matching criteria (canton/region and/or contract) to a named group, previewing the matches before applying.

steps.prompt: Ask the user into which group the matching employees should be added (the group name).
steps.description: the name of the target group to fill

steps.prompt: Ask by which criteria the employees should be selected — for example a canton/region and/or a contract name.
steps.description: the selection criteria, e.g. a canton/region and/or a contract name

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group memberships should be valid (the start date applied to all added members). Accept a concrete date like 2026-05-01 or 'today'.
steps.description: the start date (valid-from) for the new memberships

steps.note: Bulk-fill the group. groupName and validFrom are supplied — do not ask for them again. Map the user's criteria (see the known values below) to the optional parameters canton (a Swiss canton code like 'BE', or a canton name) and/or contractName. ALWAYS call with apply=false first to return a preview of who would be added; never set apply=true until the user has explicitly confirmed the previewed list. Relay the preview, and on apply relay the added and verified counts from the skill result to the user.

recipes.name: add-absence-for-employee
recipes.goal: Place an absence (Break) on an existing employee for a given date and absence type.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the full name (first and last name) of the employee the absence is for.
steps.description: the full name (first and last name) of the employee the absence is for

steps.note: Find the employee. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching employee automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user what kind of absence it is (e.g. vacation, sick, training, unpaid leave).
steps.description: the kind of absence (vacation, sick, training, ...)

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the date of the absence.
steps.description: the date of the absence as a calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd)

steps.note: List the available absence types so their IDs are known for the next step. Do not ask the user anything here; just call list_absence_types and read its result.

Place the absence now. clientId is supplied — do not ask for it. Choose absenceId from the absence types that list_absence_types just returned by matching the kind of absence the user named (see known values below). Use the date the user gave (known values) as date, formatted yyyy-MM-dd. Leave startTime, endTime and workTime at their defaults unless the user explicitly specified them.

recipes.name: bulk-add-absence-for-group
recipes.goal: Place the same absence (e.g. a training day / Schulung) on every member of a named group for a single date, previewing the affected members before applying.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the group whose members should get the absence.
steps.description: the name of the group whose members get the absence

steps.prompt: Ask the user what kind of absence it is (e.g. training/Schulung, vacation, sick).
steps.description: the kind of absence to place (e.g. Schulung, Ferien, Krank)

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the date of the absence.
steps.description: the date of the absence as a calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd)

steps.note: Bulk-place the absence on all members of the group. groupName, absenceType and date are supplied — do not ask for them. ALWAYS call with apply=false first to return a preview of how many members would get the absence; never set apply=true until the user has explicitly confirmed. Relay the preview, and on apply relay the added and verified counts from the skill result to the user.

recipes.name: create-group
recipes.goal: Create a new group (root level or nested under a parent) with a name, validity start date and holiday calendar.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the new group.
steps.description: the name of the new group

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group is valid (valid-until is optional).
steps.description: the start date from which the new group is valid

steps.prompt: Ask the user where the group should sit: at the top level (root), or nested under an existing parent group. If nested, ask for the parent group's name.
steps.description: where the group belongs: 'root' / top level, or the name of the parent group to nest it under

steps.prompt: Ask the user which holiday calendar the group should use (e.g. a canton like 'Kanton Zürich').
steps.description: the name of the holiday calendar for the group (e.g. a canton)

steps.note: Find the holiday calendar. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching calendar automatically when exactly one matches.

Look up candidate parent groups for the placement the user gave. Just call list_groups and read its result; do not ask anything. If the placement is 'root' / top level, this lookup simply finds nothing relevant — that is fine.

Create the group now. name and calendarId are supplied — do not ask for them. Use the start date the user gave (see the known values below) as validFrom, formatted yyyy-MM-dd. PLACEMENT: if the user chose root / top level / 'Wurzel', or did NOT name an existing parent group, do NOT pass parentId (the group is created at root level). ONLY if the user named an existing parent group, set parentId to that group's Id from the list_groups result above. The payment interval defaults to Monthly.

recipes.name: add-shift-to-group
recipes.goal: Add an existing shift/order to a named group.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the shift/order to add to the group.
steps.description: the name of the shift/order to add to the group

steps.note: Find the shift. The search string is supplied. The recipe captures the matching shift automatically when exactly one shift matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the group the shift should be added to.
steps.description: the name of the group the shift should be added to

steps.note: Find the target group. The search term is supplied. The recipe captures the matching group automatically when exactly one group matches.
Add the shift to the group now. shiftId and groupId are supplied — do not ask for them. This skill rejects duplicates, so it safely refuses if the shift is already in the group.

recipes.name: bulk-add-shifts-to-group
recipes.goal: Bulk-add all shifts/orders matching a search term to a named group, previewing the matches before applying.

steps.prompt: Ask the user into which group the matching shifts should be added (the group name).
steps.description: the name of the target group to fill with shifts

steps.prompt: Ask the user which shifts to add — a name or search term matched against the shift name/abbreviation.
steps.description: the search term that selects the shifts to add (matched against shift name/abbreviation)

steps.note: Bulk-link the matching shifts to the group. groupName and searchTerm are supplied — do not ask for them. ALWAYS call with apply=false first to return a preview of which shifts would be added; never set apply=true until the user has explicitly confirmed the previewed list. Relay the preview, and on apply relay the added and verified counts from the skill result to the user.

recipes.name: add-customer-to-nearest-group
recipes.goal: Add a customer to the group geographically nearest to the customer's address.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the customer to place into the nearest group.
steps.description: the name (or company) of the customer to place into the nearest group

steps.note: Find the customer. Pass the supplied search term AND entityType='Customer'. The recipe captures the matching customer automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group membership should be valid (the start date). Accept a concrete date like 2026-05-01 or 'today'.
steps.description: the start date (valid-from) for the new membership

steps.note: Place the customer into the geographically nearest group now. clientId and validFrom are supplied — do not ask for them again. The nearest group is derived from group coordinates (geocoded from group names). If the customer has no geocoded address or no group carries coordinates, the skill leaves it unchanged and explains why — relay that explanation.

recipes.name: bulk-add-customers-to-nearest-group
recipes.goal: Bulk re-assign every customer to its geographically nearest location group, previewing the proposed moves before applying.

steps.note: Re-distribute ALL customers to their geographically nearest location group. This recipe takes no per-customer input — it operates on the whole customer base. ALWAYS call propose_customer_grouping first (a read-only dry run): relay the proposed move count, the per-target-group breakdown and any customers that cannot be placed. Do NOT persist anything yet. Only after the user explicitly confirms, call apply_customer_grouping to persist the moves — that skill verifies every new membership in the database and returns added/verified counts; relay those verified counts to the user. If no group carries coordinates, tell the user to geocode the location groups first via geocode_location_groups.

recipes.name: bulk-add-employees-to-nearest-group
recipes.goal: Bulk re-assign every employee to its geographically nearest location group, previewing the proposed moves before applying.

steps.note: Re-distribute ALL employees to their geographically nearest location group. This recipe takes no per-employee input — it operates on the whole employee base. ALWAYS call propose_employee_grouping first (a read-only dry run): relay the proposed move count, the per-target-group breakdown and any employees that cannot be placed. Do NOT persist anything yet. Only after the user explicitly confirms, call apply_employee_grouping to persist the moves — that skill verifies every new membership in the database and returns added/verified counts; relay those verified counts to the user. If no group carries coordinates, tell the user to geocode the location groups first via geocode_location_groups.

recipes.name: add-extern-employee-to-nearest-group
recipes.goal: Add an external employee to the group geographically nearest to their address.

steps.prompt: Ask the user for the name of the external employee to place into the nearest group.
steps.description: the full name of the external employee to place into the nearest group

steps.note: Find the external employee. Pass the supplied search term AND entityType='ExternEmp'. The recipe captures the matching external employee automatically when exactly one matches.

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group membership should be valid (the start date). Accept a concrete date like 2026-05-01 or 'today'.
steps.description: the start date (valid-from) for the new membership

steps.note: Place the external employee into the geographically nearest group now. clientId and validFrom are supplied — do not ask for them again. The nearest group is derived from group coordinates (geocoded from group names). If the person has no geocoded address or no group carries coordinates, the skill leaves it unchanged and explains why — relay that explanation.

recipes.name: add-selected-clients-to-group
recipes.goal: Add the clients the user ticked in the list (the UI checkbox selection) to a named group, previewing who would be added before applying.

steps.prompt: Ask the user into which group the selected employees/clients should be added (the group name).
steps.description: the name of the target group the selected clients should be added to

steps.prompt: Ask the user from which date the new group memberships should be valid (the start date applied to all selected members). Accept a concrete date like 2026-05-01 or 'today'.
steps.description: the start date (valid-from) for the new memberships

steps.note: Add the clients the user ticked in the list to the group. groupName and validFrom are supplied — do not ask for them again. The selected client ids come from the current UI selection (the execution context), NOT from a parameter — never ask the user for them. ALWAYS call with apply=false first to return a preview of who would be added; never set apply=true until the user has explicitly confirmed the previewed list. Relay the preview, and on apply relay the added and verified counts from the skill result to the user.
